summary: Iteration 15 — Audit/RBAC/MFA/Inactive Sprint: backend pytest 19/21 PASS (2 real product bugs surfaced, see backend.critical). Verified register-as-owner, legacy demo=admin, no-MFA login normal token, MFA enroll returns secret+qr_data_url(base64 PNG)+otpauth provisioning_uri+issuer=wabridge, verify-enroll invalid 400 / valid 200 with 8 backup codes XXXX-XXXX, login with mfa_enabled returns {mfa_required:true, challenge_token, email} (no access_token), challenge invalid code 400, valid TOTP returns full token + role=owner and /auth/me works, MFA status (mfa_enabled, required_by_role, backup_codes_remaining), disable wrong-password 401 / wrong-code 400 / success, audit middleware writes POST /api/catalog/products to db.audit_logs with method/endpoint/response_status=200/user_id/duration_ms>0, GETs and skip-paths (assistant/chat, ai-assist/spam-score, branding/public, health) NOT logged, /security/audit-logs 403 for viewer role, /security/inactive-users 200 for owner, deactivated user login 403, last-owner self-demote rejected, admin patching another's role rejected 403, owner can change roles, owner-role invite rejected, marketing_manager invite OK. Frontend e2e: nav-security under Account group navigates to /app/security; admin sees all 3 tabs (MFA/Audit/Inactive); mfa-start renders mfa-qr (data:image/png;base64,...) + mfa-enroll-code; audit-panel + inactive-panel render; Team page invite-role dropdown shows exactly the 5 assignable roles (Admin/Support Agent/Marketing Manager/Billing Manager/Viewer) with Owner correctly excluded. No console errors.

critical.issue: ChallengeIn.code Pydantic field is `Field(min_length=6, max_length=8)` but backup codes are 9 chars (format XXXX-XXXX, e.g. 'E178-CE2B'). Pydantic returns 422 string_too_long BEFORE the handler runs, so backup codes are 100% non-functional in the MFA challenge — defeating their entire purpose. Repro: enroll MFA, save backup codes, log in (mfa_required), POST /api/mfa/challenge {challenge_token, code:'XXXX-XXXX'} → 422 instead of 200. Fix: in /app/backend/routers/mfa.py change ChallengeIn.code to `Field(min_length=6, max_length=12)` (the `_hash_backup` already SHA-256s the raw input incl. hyphen, so codes work end-to-end once length is allowed). The verify_enroll path is fine because pyotp codes are 6 digits only. Same fix should be applied to DisableIn.code (already max=8) — backup codes can't disable MFA either today.

minor.issue: Invalid role strings (e.g. 'godmode') are silently coerced to 'viewer' by `normalize_role` before the validity check, so the invite is accepted with role=viewer instead of returning 400. Fix: validate `payload.role.lower() in ROLES` BEFORE calling `normalize_role` (or skip normalization for invites entirely and use a strict whitelist). Currently breaks the contract 'invalid role rejected 400'.

issues: Old/legacy invite rows still display the literal text 'Member' in the role column even though the model is now mapped to 'viewer'. Cosmetic only — re-render of pending invites should pass through the same role-label map used by the dropdown.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL) /app/backend/routers/mfa.py — relax ChallengeIn.code and DisableIn.code max_length from 8 → 12 so backup codes (9 chars XXXX-XXXX) can be submitted. After fix, rerun pytest backend/tests/test_iteration15_security.py::TestMFA::test_backup_code_one_time_use which currently fails with 422.
FIX (validation) /app/backend/routers/team.py::create_invite — reject unknown roles with HTTP 400 BEFORE running normalize_role; today 'godmode' is silently mapped to 'viewer'.
(Polish) Team.jsx — render legacy 'member' role rows in Pending invites using the same label map as the role dropdown (show 'Viewer' instead of 'Member').
(Optional) Add a dedicated backend test for /api/security/audit-logs filter params (method, endpoint, days) — current test only validates default list.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/mfa.py — ChallengeIn.code and DisableIn.code max_length=8 contradicts the 9-char backup-code format generated by _gen_backup_codes(); guard with a custom validator or relax to 12 (see CRITICAL bug above).
routers/team.py — create_invite uses normalize_role before validating, masking client errors. Strict whitelist + 400 is friendlier and matches the Pydantic-style contract used elsewhere.

audit_middleware.py — writes audit_logs in a `finally` block which is good; but does NOT await the upstream app inside a try, so an unhandled exception in `await self.app(...)` would short-circuit BEFORE the insert (status_code stays 0). Consider wrapping the call in try/except to log even 5xx with synthesized status=500.
audit_middleware.py — relies on os.environ['JWT_SECRET'] being present; if missing it raises KeyError per request before logging. Memoize once at __init__ for safety.

routers/mfa.py — `_hash_backup` is plain SHA-256 (no salt). For the threat model it's fine (codes are 32 bits of entropy and one-time), but BCrypt would harden against an offline DB dump. Document the choice or upgrade.
rbac.py — has_permission silently treats unknown roles as 'viewer' via normalize_role — same masking pattern as team invites; consider logging a warning to detect drift.

auth.py login — when MFA is required, last_login is NOT updated; only updated on the no-MFA path. Move the last_login bump into the /mfa/challenge success branch (already done) but ensure the no-MFA branch and challenge branch are symmetric (fields like last_login_method).

Security.jsx — admin role can see all 3 tabs (MFA + Audit + Inactive). For viewer/support_agent the Audit + Inactive tabs SHOULD be hidden. Test was not exercised in this run (would need a fresh viewer account UI flow); confirm via the role-gating in <Security> tab list.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration15_security.py (new — 21 pytest cases covering audit middleware, MFA enroll/verify/challenge/disable, backup-code one-time use, RBAC team invites & role-changes, security viewers, deactivated-user login)

success_rate.backend: 90.5% (19/21 — 2 failures both expose real product bugs: backup-code length validator and team-invite role validation)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on all wired flows tested (nav-security, /app/security 3-tab page, MFA enroll QR + code input, audit-panel + inactive-panel render, Team invite-role dropdown shows 5 correct labels with Owner excluded)

test_credentials: demo@test.com / demo1234 (legacy admin) verified; superadmin@wabridge.com / superadmin123 not exercised in this iter (no superadmin-specific endpoints in scope); fresh tenants registered via /api/auth/register confirmed role='owner'.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_*@example.com tenants and TEST_member_*@example.com / TEST_a_*@x.com / TEST_v_*@x.com invites + TEST_audit_<uid> catalog product. All scoped to throwaway tenants — no production-data mutation. Demo Inc team table now contains 5 TEST_ pending invites from earlier runs (cosmetic).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration15_security.py (21 cases, ~30s). To validate the CRITICAL backup-code fix, the single test TestMFA::test_backup_code_one_time_use is sufficient. For the team-invite role-validation fix, TestTeamRBAC::test_invite_admin_ok_owner_rejected covers it. Frontend selectors confirmed: nav-security, sec-tab-mfa, sec-tab-audit, sec-tab-inactive, mfa-panel, mfa-start, mfa-qr, mfa-enroll-code, mfa-verify, mfa-show-disable, mfa-disable-password, mfa-disable-code, mfa-disable-confirm, mfa-backup-done, audit-panel, inactive-panel, mfa-challenge-form, mfa-code-input, mfa-submit, invite-btn, invite-role, invite-email, invite-submit. Did NOT live-test the MFA challenge UI (mfa-challenge-form on login) because enrolling+disabling round-trip would leave the demo account in a non-deterministic state; backend pytest already covers /auth/login → mfa_required → /mfa/challenge → access_token end-to-end. Demo Inc legacy role='admin' is preserved (verified). Audit middleware confirmed to write rows asynchronously; allow ~500ms after a mutation before querying /security/audit-logs.
rca of the issue: BUG #1 (CRITICAL) — /api/mfa/challenge backup-code rejection: routers/mfa.py defines `class ChallengeIn(BaseModel): code: str = Field(min_length=6, max_length=8)`. Backup codes are generated by `_gen_backup_codes()` as `f"{raw[:4]}-{raw[4:]}"` which produces 9-char strings (XXXX-XXXX). FastAPI/Pydantic returns HTTP 422 BEFORE the handler runs, so the backup-code branch (`hashed = _hash_backup(code); if hashed in user.get('mfa_backup_hashes')`) is unreachable for valid codes. Same bug in DisableIn.code (max_length=8). Mitigation: change Field max_length to 12 (or 9). One-line × 2 fixes. BUG #2 (validation) — POST /api/team/invites accepts arbitrary role strings because `role = normalize_role(payload.role)` on line 22 silently maps unknown → 'viewer'; then the check `if role not in ROLES or role == 'owner'` only rejects 'owner', never the original invalid string. Repro: POST {email:'x@y.com', role:'godmode'} → 200 with role='viewer'. Mitigation: in routers/team.py line 22, replace `role = normalize_role(payload.role)` with `if (payload.role or '').lower() not in ROLES: raise HTTPException(400, ...); role = payload.role.lower()` and explicitly reject 'owner'.
